title: Koi Pond

jokes.text: We are doing a haunted house this halloween, which is actually kind of spooky, because, as legend has it, on this very site there used to be a productive paper company.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about the haunted house being spooky
jokes.punchline: The real horror is that this used to be a productive workplace
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall while staying in character. Self-deprecating company joke that's both clever and earned.

jokes.text: Look, nobody told me what people were, all right? So label yourselves or take what you get.
jokes.setup: Michael describing costumes incorrectly
jokes.punchline: His defensive justification for not knowing what anyone is supposed to be
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael deflection and poor management skills on display in a low-stakes situation

jokes.text: I vant to sell your blood! That's really not the trend in vampires right now.
jokes.setup: Michael doing vampire voice
jokes.punchline: Oscar's dry commentary on vampire cultural trends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar character moment - pedantic correction about pop culture while Michael tries to be scary

jokes.text: I'm jigsaw, idiot. You're not as scary as bookface over there. Yes, I am the popular social networking site known as bookface.
jokes.setup: Costume confusion and competition
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact explanation of his nonsensical Facebook costume
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Creed moment with the wrong social media name delivered completely seriously

jokes.text: Kids, just remember, suicide is never the answer, all right? Why is christmas the only holiday that can have a message?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to give Halloween a meaningful message
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate suicide PSA at a children's Halloween party
jokes.explanation: Hilariously inappropriate Michael moment - wanting to be meaningful but spectacularly missing the mark with children present

jokes.text: I can't believe it's yogurt. Oh, it'll be fun.
jokes.setup: Discussion about TCBY
jokes.punchline: Michael messing up the 'I can't believe it's not butter' reference
jokes.explanation: Mild Michael malapropism but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Jim is a good kid. He can handle a lot, but sometimes you have to call in a master. Why would you date an amateur when you could date a professional?
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he needs to accompany Jim
jokes.punchline: The bizarre dating analogy for sales calls
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate analogy showing his lack of self-awareness about mentoring

jokes.text: All right. All right. Let me go get your stroller.
jokes.setup: Jim saying he doesn't want to feel babied
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal baby reference
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the babysitting metaphor but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Sure, michael's a good teacher. A teacher is someone who stands right next to you your whole life and never lets you do anything. That's what a teacher is, right?
jokes.setup: Jim's talking head about Michael as a teacher
jokes.punchline: The definition of a teacher as someone who never lets you do anything
jokes.explanation: Good dry Jim observation with subtle sarcasm about Michael's micromanaging

jokes.text: It is a tankard. I highly recommend you wear that.
jokes.setup: Michael offering Jim his watch
jokes.punchline: Calling a watch a 'tankard' (which is a drinking vessel)
jokes.explanation: Mild malapropism showing Michael trying to sound sophisticated but getting the word wrong

jokes.text: He's trying to micro-co-manage me. Or co-micromanage... Me.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to describe Michael's behavior
jokes.punchline: Getting confused by his own made-up compound words
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay moment showing Jim's frustration through verbal fumbling

jokes.text: Yeah, I was gonna sing that part. I know. Now you don't have to. Well, except it was gonna resolve the melody, so... Now my head hurts. Feels like I held in a sneeze.
jokes.setup: Andy wanting to sing harmony
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic reaction to musical incompleteness
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his musical pretensions and dramatic overreaction

jokes.text: Why isn't jim wet? I... Outran it.
jokes.setup: Michael is soaking wet from alleged flash rain
jokes.punchline: Jim's ridiculous explanation that he outran the rain
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan Jim response with absurd logic delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I don't think it rained. My hip would be throbbing.
jokes.setup: Michael insisting it rained
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact weather prediction method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley deadpan with his characteristic old-man complaints used as fact

jokes.text: Looks like somebody's got a case of the 'definitelys.'
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam repeatedly saying 'definitely not' about being together
jokes.punchline: Pam's playful observation about their overuse of 'definitely'
jokes.explanation: Clever linguistic observation turned into a fake medical condition format

jokes.text: Did you say kol pond? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Erin mentions Michael's keys were found in the koi pond
jokes.punchline: Michael's attempt to mishear 'koi' as 'kol'
jokes.explanation: Setup for the koi pond running gag with Michael's futile attempt at damage control

jokes.text: Maybe I was trying to save a child that had fallen in. So a child had fallen in? Not yet!
jokes.setup: Michael making up increasingly elaborate stories about the pond
jokes.punchline: His admission that he was preventing a hypothetical future child drowning
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Michael's desperation to spin the story positively with absurd logic

jokes.text: Truthfully, it wasn't the way he fell in. It was how long it took him to get out.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Michael falling in the pond
jokes.punchline: The implication that Michael struggled to exit the pond
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim observation that makes the embarrassment worse through understatement

jokes.text: I date models. Face models. My girlfriend, on a scale of one to gisele, uh, a nine.
jokes.setup: Client thinking Andy and Pam are a couple
jokes.punchline: Andy's over-the-top compensation with specific modeling references
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy overcompensation with needlessly specific details that make it less believable

jokes.text: Pam's carrying our surrogate, because my girlfriend needed to keep her figure for fashion week, so we, uh, we put our baby in pam.
jokes.setup: Andy continuing to lie about his relationship status
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and inappropriate lie about Pam being a surrogate
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's lie with uncomfortable details about using Pam

jokes.text: You were way meaner to me than I was to you. The very idea of us together made you burst out laughing like you just bit into an adam sandler and jelly sandwich.
jokes.setup: Pam and Andy arguing about who was meaner
jokes.punchline: Pam's specific and absurd sandwich metaphor for something unpalatable
jokes.explanation: Creative and specific metaphor for something distasteful with good pop culture reference

jokes.text: Staples?
jokes.setup: Michael asking for clips that hold paper together
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent confusion about whether he means staples
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Erin's literal-mindedness and helpfulness

jokes.text: He heard you made a big splash at the meeting.
jokes.setup: David Wallace calling about the meeting
jokes.punchline: 'Big splash' pun referencing the koi pond incident
jokes.explanation: Obvious but effective pun that connects to the main storyline

jokes.text: A carp is both a fish and a term for complaining. They're mocking you with wordplay!
jokes.setup: Stanley telling Michael to ignore their 'carping'
jokes.punchline: Oscar's pedantic explanation of the double meaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar moment - unable to let wordplay go unexplained even when it ruins the joke

jokes.text: When you fell in, did you flounder? Good one, phyllis. Michael, flounder is both a kind of a fish...
jokes.setup: Phyllis asking if Michael floundered
jokes.punchline: The fish/struggling double meaning, with Oscar starting to explain again
jokes.explanation: Solid pun with Oscar characteristically starting to over-explain again

jokes.text: I'm not usually the butt of the joke. I'm usually the face of the joke.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on being mocked
jokes.punchline: His confused metaphor about being the 'face' rather than 'butt' of jokes
jokes.explanation: Good Michael confusion about idioms while trying to maintain dignity

jokes.text: I wish jim had fallen into that pond. Then he'd have to put on my suit, and it would be too short, and he'd look... Damn it, he'd still look good.
jokes.setup: Michael wishing Jim had fallen in instead
jokes.punchline: His reluctant admission that Jim would still look good in an ill-fitting suit
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Michael's jealousy and grudging respect for Jim

jokes.text: Show of hands. Who here has been kol-ponded?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to make a general point about sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Turning his specific embarrassment into a made-up verb
jokes.explanation: Good Michael attempt to universalize his specific humiliation

jokes.text: Well, it is hard to tell the difference between you guys saying stop because I want you to stop, or stop as in, 'stop! You're making me laugh so hard. What you're doing is so funny. you're on a roll, I am busting a gut. Stop!'
jokes.setup: Employees saying they've complained about Michael's jokes before
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate misinterpretation of their protests as encouragement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion and self-justification showing his complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: I don't want people making fun of my nose. Your nose? It's too small.
jokes.setup: Dwight sharing something for the do not mock list
jokes.punchline: His insecurity about having a small nose instead of big
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations - usually people worry about big noses, not small ones

jokes.text: Oh, my, that is small. Can you breathe okay? What keeps your glasses on? Did you sneeze it off?
jokes.setup: Michael noticing Dwight's small nose
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly absurd questions about nose functionality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of nose jokes showing Michael can't help himself even during sensitivity training

jokes.text: Well, I really didn't want to put it on the board, but I thought maybe it was gonna come out somehow, so...
jokes.setup: Meredith writing something embarrassing on the board privately
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact acceptance that her embarrassing secret would be revealed anyway
jokes.explanation: Good Meredith character moment with typical resignation about her personal issues

jokes.text: Well, you two are quite the salesmen, and a very cute couple. Thank you. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Client commenting on Andy and Pam as a sales team
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately accepting the couple compliment instead of correcting
jokes.explanation: Setup for the fake relationship bit showing Andy's eagerness to play along

jokes.text: Right, sometimes i'll just dance for hours in the living room... And I'll watch him.
jokes.setup: Andy and Pam improvising as a fake couple
jokes.punchline: The increasingly weird details about their 'relationship' including solo dancing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake relationship with increasingly uncomfortable details

jokes.text: This sounds ridiculous, I know, but some people say that I eat like a squirrel.
jokes.setup: Kevin adding to the do not mock list
jokes.punchline: His complaint about being compared to a cute animal
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment with his typical food-related insecurity

jokes.text: Apparently, a kol has died. It's a fish. They want you to pay for it. It could have died of natural causes, though. Well, they said you stepped on its head.
jokes.setup: News about the koi pond situation getting worse
jokes.punchline: Michael's hopeful suggestion undercut by the specific cause of death
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect timing on the punchline that destroys Michael's hope

jokes.text: He did not suffer. Um, when is the funeral?
jokes.setup: Learning he killed a fish
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate concern about funeral arrangements for a koi
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael response showing his misplaced priorities and guilt about the fish

jokes.text: I could get a fish for a five-cent worm. Oh, you're paying way too much for worms, then. Who's your worm guy?
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about the $300 fish replacement cost
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact expertise about worm pricing and suppliers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Creed moment - completely missing the point while having oddly specific expertise about worm costs

jokes.text: Hey, li'l soybean, what do you want? It... I felt a kick. Oh, that's great. Oh, my gosh, like a little magical foot just high-fived me.
jokes.setup: Andy talking to Pam's 'baby' during their fake relationship act
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top emotional reaction to feeling the baby kick
jokes.explanation: Good Andy over-enthusiasm with the 'magical foot high-five' description being particularly excessive

jokes.text: It's like he's trying to say, 'i love you, too, daddy.' I love you, too.
jokes.setup: Andy continuing to get emotional about the fake baby
jokes.punchline: His response to the 'baby' calling him daddy
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Andy's over-investment in the fake relationship scenario

jokes.text: Kids in high school still call me ponytail. No, they don't. Yes, they do, jim. Because of the time I got my ponytail stuck in the power drill.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming lasting embarrassment from high school
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous backstory about getting his ponytail caught in a power drill
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd Michael backstory that's both specific and completely believable for his character

jokes.text: In high school, the girl's volleyball team always used to throw me into the frozen lake. Four years in a row.
jokes.setup: Michael sharing more embarrassing stories
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about it happening annually for his entire high school career
jokes.explanation: Good Michael oversharing with the pathetic detail about it being a tradition

jokes.text: And the guy was like, 'who are your five friends?' and I'm like, 'uh...' I didn't even know! I couldn't even think.
jokes.setup: Michael's phone plan story
jokes.punchline: His inability to name five friends for the friends and family plan
jokes.explanation: Painfully relatable and sad Michael moment that reveals his loneliness in a specific, believable way

jokes.text: I don't even have jan's cell phone number, and I hate her! She won't give it to me. It's like, oh, well, I guess I'm a loser. A loo-hoo-hoo-hoo-ser!
jokes.setup: Michael's continued spiral about having no friends
jokes.punchline: His pathetic singing breakdown calling himself a loser
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael meltdown with the sung 'loser' being both funny and deeply sad

jokes.text: Enjoying your nut? Why? I'm not mocking. I was just making an observation about her... Nut.
jokes.setup: Kevin eating nuts after the sensitivity training
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's innocent question becoming potentially mockable under the new rules
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the squirrel comment showing how the sensitivity training backfired

jokes.text: If I'm being completely honest, I could have done without the belly kiss. You know what? I'm sorry, 'cause in that moment, I knew I was kissing your belly too much.
jokes.setup: Pam and Andy debriefing their fake couple performance
jokes.punchline: Andy's admission of excessive fake belly kissing
jokes.explanation: Andy's self-awareness about crossing boundaries while still having crossed them

jokes.text: Jim, you let michael fall in. He purposefully leaned away and let you fall.
jokes.setup: Watching the security footage
jokes.punchline: Oscar pointing out that Jim deliberately didn't help Michael
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that adds new dimension to the pond incident showing Jim's complicity

jokes.text: Jim is my enemy, but it turns out that jim is also his own worst enemy. And the enemy of my enemy is my friend, so jim is actually my friend. But because he is his own worst enemy, the enemy of my friend is my enemy, so actually, jim is my enemy. But...
jokes.setup: Michael trying to figure out his relationship with Jim
jokes.punchline: Getting completely tangled in the logic of 'enemy of my enemy'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic pretzel that perfectly captures his overthinking and confusion while being genuinely clever

jokes.text: Jim is jealous of me? Jim is jealous of me.
jokes.setup: Jim admitting he wanted to do the sales call alone
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely wrong interpretation that Jim is jealous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael misreading of the situation showing his narcissistic interpretation of events

jokes.text: Oscar's a douche. Oh, jim, I think I'm in your way. No, he's all right. No, he's a... Yeah, he's all right.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Oscar a douche right as Oscar appears
jokes.punchline: Michael's awkward attempt to cover and Jim's diplomatic response
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward timing with Michael getting caught talking behind someone's back

jokes.text: He's like marlon brando. Oh. Do you mean marlon wayans? 'cause he is. I actually do mean marlon wayans, yeah.
jokes.setup: Erin comparing Andy to Marlon Brando
jokes.punchline: Her immediate agreement when corrected to the much less impressive Marlon Wayans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment showing her lack of cultural knowledge while trying to compliment Andy

tmdb_overview: Michael and Jim head off to a meeting and Michael comes back soaked after having fallen in a koi pond. Meanwhile, Pam and Andy, as the two worst salesmen in the branch, have to go cold calling for sales.
